metadata.title: Water Treatment Plant Cyber Attack Scenario
metadata.author: Cybersecurity Analyst

overview.Summary: A sophisticated cyber attack on a water treatment plant, leveraging spear phishing and exploitation of vulnerabilities in various systems.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2023-38035 (Suggested): Ivanti Sentry, formerly known as MobileIron Sentry, contains an authentication bypass vulnerability that may allow an attacker to bypass authentication controls on the administrative interface due to an insufficiently restrictive Apache HTTPD configuration.
stage.description: The attacker sends a spear-phishing email to a water treatment plant employee, tricking them into revealing their login credentials, which are then used to gain access to the plant's internal network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2023-41990 (Suggested): Apple iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, and watchOS contain an unspecified vulnerability that allows for code execution when processing a font file.
stage.description: The attacker uses the compromised credentials to establish a remote connection to the SCADA system, exploiting the vulnerability to execute arbitrary code.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2022-33891 (Suggested): Apache Spark contains a command injection vulnerability via Spark User Interface (UI) when Access Control Lists (ACLs) are enabled.
stage.description: The attacker injects malicious code into the control system software, allowing them to manipulate the water quality parameters.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker causes a denial-of-service condition by manipulating the network traffic, effectively disabling the water treatment plant's ability to function properly.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: The attacker steals sensitive information about the water treatment process by executing commands on the compromised system.

Scenario.summary: This attack scenario demonstrates a sophisticated cyber attack on a water treatment plant, leveraging spear phishing and exploitation of vulnerabilities in various systems to disrupt operations and steal sensitive information.
